feed.title: SAP Community - ABAP Development
feed.subtitle: ABAP Development Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Assistance Required: OData API Errors on SAP CAL FPS01 2025
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community, I am currently testing API integrations on an SAP CAL FPS01 2025 system and have encountered blockers with three standard OData services. I have done some initial troubleshooting but need guidance on standard resolutions or missing configurations.

Financial Planning API

When attempting a POST request to API_FINPLANNINGDATA_SRV, I receive a message stating the CREATE_ENTITY method is not implemented in the data provider class. I suspect this requires ABAP intervention in the DPC_EXT class under CO-FIO-OM-PL. Is there a standard SAP Note to regenerate the runtime objects for this service, or is manual ABAP implementation strictly required to enable POST operations?

Fixed Asset API

I cannot locate the Fixed Asset APIs in the OData V2 catalog, though I see API_FIXEDASSETACQUISITION_G4BA exists in OData V4. Have all Fixed Asset APIs strictly migrated to V4 in the 2025 release? If not, is there a recommended V2 alternative that does not require building Custom CDS Views as an External API?

Enterprise Project API

Posting to version 2 of API_ENTERPRISE_PROJECT_SRV results in an ABAP short dump OBJECTS_OBJREF_NOT_ASSIGNED_NO due to a dereferenced null object. I believe this is caused by stricter mandatory field requirements in version 2 compared to version 1. Could someone provide the absolute minimal working JSON/XML payload required for v2, or point me to a patch note for this specific dump?

API Status Matrix

 
Service Name Error Encountered Suspected Resolution Path
API_FINPLANNINGDATA_SRVCREATE_ENTITY not implementedSEGW regeneration or ABAP developer overriding DPC_EXT
Fixed Asset APINot found in OData V2 catalogSwitch client integration to OData V4 endpoints
API_ENTERPRISE_PROJECT_SRVOBJECTS_OBJREF_NOT_ASSIGNED_NOCorrecting missing payload fields or applying SAP Note

entry.title: How to get before image of attachments in FWO?
entry.content:
Hi,

While working on a requirement we encountered a strange situation.

We are trying to read the before image attachment data in FWO. When a new attachment is created, before image works fine and it is possible to see the already existing attachments, also their file_content data. But when an existing attachment is removed, we cannot see the content file in before image.

We are coding inside the determination class (before save) for TRQ objects. Any idea why?

Here is the code we are using.

"Read attachment keys of this Forwarding Order.
io_read->retrieve_by_association(
EXPORTING
iv_node = /scmtms/if_trq_c=>sc_node-root
it_key = it_key
iv_association = /scmtms/if_trq_c=>sc_association-root-attachmentfolder
IMPORTING
et_target_key = DATA(lit_trq_att_root_key) ).

"Read the node key and association key to be able to read document data from dependent object.
/scmtms/cl_common_helper=>get_do_keys_4_rba(
EXPORTING
iv_host_bo_key = /scmtms/if_trq_c=>sc_bo_key
iv_host_do_node_key = /scmtms/if_trq_c=>sc_node-attachmentfolder
iv_do_node_key = /bobf/if_attachment_folder_c=>sc_node-root
iv_do_assoc_key = /bobf/if_attachment_folder_c=>sc_association-root-document_list
IMPORTING
ev_node_key = DATA(lfd_atf_node_key_doc)
ev_assoc_key = DATA(lfd_atf_assoc_key_doc) ).

"Read the attachment documents data of before image.
io_read->retrieve_by_association( 
EXPORTING
iv_node = lfd_atf_node_key_doc
it_key = lit_trq_att_root_key
iv_association = lfd_atf_assoc_key_doc
iv_before_image = abap_true
iv_fill_data = abap_true
IMPORTING
et_data = lit_trq_att_doc_bi ).

"Read the node key and association key to be able to read file content data from dependent object.
/scmtms/cl_common_helper=>get_do_keys_4_rba(
EXPORTING
iv_host_bo_key = /scmtms/if_trq_c=>sc_bo_key
iv_host_do_node_key = /scmtms/if_trq_c=>sc_node-attachmentfolder
iv_do_node_key = /bobf/if_attachment_folder_c=>sc_node-document
iv_do_assoc_key = /bobf/if_attachment_folder_c=>sc_association-document-file_content
IMPORTING
ev_node_key = DATA(lfd_atf_node_key_cont)
ev_assoc_key = DATA(lfd_atf_assoc_key_cont) ).

"Read the file content data of before image.
io_read->retrieve_by_association( 
EXPORTING
iv_node = lfd_atf_node_key_cont
it_key = CORRESPONDING #( lit_trq_att_doc_bi MAPPING key = key )
iv_association = lfd_atf_assoc_key_cont
iv_before_image = abap_true
iv_fill_data = abap_true
IMPORTING
et_data = lit_content_bi ).

"Read the document data of current image.
io_read->retrieve_by_association( 
EXPORTING
iv_node = lfd_atf_node_key_doc
it_key = lit_trq_att_root_key
iv_association = lfd_atf_assoc_key_doc
iv_fill_data = abap_true
IMPORTING
et_data = lit_trq_att_document ).

"Read the file content data of current image.
io_read->retrieve_by_association( 
EXPORTING
iv_node = lfd_atf_node_key_cont
it_key = CORRESPONDING #( lit_trq_att_document MAPPING key = key )
iv_association = lfd_atf_assoc_key_cont
iv_fill_data = abap_true
IMPORTING
et_data = lit_content ).

entry.title: How to read FILE_CONTENT before image?
entry.content:
Hi, we have a determination class (before save) for the Forwarding Orders. (/SCMTMS/TRQ).

We encountered a wierd situation. When we add a new attachment in the FWO UI, it is possible to see the before image with true data in the determination class. But when removing an existing attachment, file_content of the removed attachment is not available in before image. Please share info about this problem.

Here is the code snippet:

entry.title: Encrypt Excel file with Password in SAP before sending as a mail attachment
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I have a requirement to develop a custom report that encrypts an Excel file (.xlsx) with a password before sending it from SAP as an email attachment.

I would like to know whether SAP provides any standard objects such as function modules or classes that can help achieve this.

So far, I have explored solutions involving the import of the ABAP2XLSX library from GitHub.

However, I would like to know whether there is any standard SAP functionality available for this requirement.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Why the idoc hierarchy are different in KAP & K4Q system. How to correct them?
entry.content:
Hi gurus,
im getting one syntax error inbound idoc in quality (K4Q-S4hana) system. because of the segment - E1EDP02 is used 5 times and max allowed is 4 times. But when compared to the PRD (KAP-ECC) system, i found there also they have used the same segment for 5 times - but you can see there 4 segment are nested under parent segment - E1IDPU1 & 1 segment is nested under another same  parent segment - E1IDPU1 (See KAP screenshot). We have migrated to the s4hane from ecc. for ecc - working fine, but s4hana - not working.

KAP:

 


K4Q:

 

TC- WE30:
Below WE30 screenshot, segment is also same in both KAP & K4Q system.

 

Now i want to know that how this structure / hierarchy is defined or created?
i want to find the root cause why this hierarchy is different in K4Q (s4) system? & how to fix it?

if you need any info please ask me....
This is little urgent..
Thnx in adv!

entry.title: RAP - Behavior projection - Error on activating Behavior projection
entry.content:
Hi All,  

Managed scenario - Created three levels of RAP BO.

Behavior definition is successfully activated however could not able to activate the "Behavior Projection".  Follwing is the error under ADT Problem - 

The association "_CUSTOMER" cannot be used as a transactional association since the target entity "ZSRA_R_CUSTTP" is not redirected to "ZSRA_C_CUST" in the CDS definition.

define root view entity  ZSRA_C_CUST
 provider contract transactional_query
  as projection on  ZSRA_CUSTTP
{
  key CustomerId,
      CustomerName,
      CreatedBy,
      CreatedAt,
      LastChangedBy,
      ChdLocalLastChangedAt,
      LastChangedAt,
      /* Assoziationen */
      _project : redirected to composition child ZSRA_C_PRJ
}

------------

define view entity ZSRA_C_PRJ
  as projection on ZSRA_R _PRJTP
{
  key CustomerId,
  key ProjectId,
      ProjectName,
      Project,
      ProjectType,
      Data,
      Exporter,
      CreatedBy,
      CreatedAt,
      LastChangedBy,
      PrjLocalLastChangedAt,
      LastChangedAt,
      /* Associations */
      _customer : redirected to parent /OSGMBH/ ZSRA_C_CUST,
      _sys: redirected to composition child ZSRA_C_SYS

}

 ------------
define view entity ZSRA_C_SYS as projection on ZSRA_ R_SYS
{
    key ProjectId,
key SystemId,
    key CustomerId,
    SystemName,
    Service,
    Tenant,
    CreatedBy,
    CreatedAt,
    LastChangedBy,
    SysLocalLastChangedAt,
    LastChangedAt,
    /* Associations */
    _project: redirected to parent  ZSRA_C_PRJ,
     _customer
}

Behavior Projection –

projection;
strict ( 2 );
use draft;
define behavior for ZSRA_C_CUST alias Customer
{
  use create;
  use update;
  use delete;
  use action Edit;
  use action Activate;
  use action Discard;
use action Resume;
  use action Prepare;
use association _project { create; with draft; }
}

define behavior for ZSRA_C_PRJ alias Project
{
  use update;
  use delete;
  use association _customer { with draft; }
  use association _sys { create; with draft; }
}
define behavior for ZSRA_C_SYS alias System
{
  use update;
  use delete;
  use association _project { with draft; }
  use association _customer { with draft; } <-- Error
}

Thanks & regards

Shobhan Rana

entry.title: Need to change custom fields in the sales order without calling change sales order BAPI
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

We have a requirement like this:

There are 2 custom fields in the sales order: one at the header level and one at the item level. These are status fields and get updated to a value based on the status of that sales order. Eg. If a sales order is delivered and PGI'ed but not invoiced, it will have a status of H7 - Delivered. Similarly, once the invoice is created, it will have a status of H9 - Invoiced. 

This functionality has been implemented and is working as designed.

There is an issue with Mexican sales orders. For such sales orders, there are 2 custom pricing conditions created with a custom routine. The issue is that even after the invoice has been created, when someone goes into VA02 and manually changes anything on the sales order (eg, header text), it triggers this custom routine and reprices the sales order. As there is now a difference between the price on the sales order and the price on the invoice, this is creating an issue.

When saving the invoice, in order to update the custom field in the sales order header, the change sales order BAPI is being called. However, this BAPI call is also triggering the custom routine, triggering a price change. Our teams are checking to resolve this issue, but a root cause hasn't been found yet. The solution also isn't clear as of now. 

We are looking for ways to update the custom field in the sales order (header and item level) without calling the change sales order BAPI, without doing a direct table udpate.

Can someone please suggest a way to achieve this.

Edit: We are using SAP S/4 HANA on Premise 2023

entry.title: Suitable Practice System for GUI based ABAP development
entry.content:
Hi fellow SAP Learners and Developers,

I am Shakthi Pranesh, and recently I got verified and got access to SAP Learning Hub Student edition subscription, and I am utilise it to learn ABAP development.

I am able to find the practice systems for ABAP development for S/HANA, ADT and RAP.

The problem I am facing is, I could not find a suitable Practice System for GUI based ABAP development, I need GUI to learn few things from it and I wish to do some experiments and practice in that system.

So please help me to find the right practice system at SAP Learning Hub which suitable for GUI based ABAP development, if you already know any please let me know.

Thanks in Advance for your help.

entry.title: After upgrading to S/4HANA, a Z-field in the purchase order header gets cleared in the database.
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We recently upgraded from ECC to S/4HANA in RISE. Following the upgrade, we encounter the following issue: when running ME22N, changing a standard field value in the purchase order header, and clicking SAVE, a custom Z-field located in the Customer Data tab (header level) gets cleared (reset to initial) in the database.

The system implements the BAdI ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST.

In the method IF_EX_ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST~FIELDSELECTION_HEADER, all fields are defined as modifiable except for this Z-field:

method IF_EX_ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST~FIELDSELECTION_HEADER.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <fs> LIKE LINE OF ch_fieldselection.

READ TABLE ch_fieldselection ASSIGNING <fs> WITH TABLE KEY metafield = mmmfd_cust_01.

IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL. "10

<fs>-fieldstatus = '.'. " Input

ENDIF.

Even if we comment out the IF statement on line 10 above—making the Z-field modifiable as well—the Z-field still gets cleared (and a message is received stating that the field cannot be changed).

Originally, this Z-field was updated inside the method IF_EX_ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST~PROCESS_HEADER. However, even after deleting the update code in method process_header, the field still resets in the database.

METHOD if_ex_me_process_po_cust~process_header.

DATA: ls_head TYPE mepoheader,

lv_zolla TYPE lfm1-zolla,

ls_lfa1 TYPE lfa1.

INCLUDE mm_messages_mac.

ls_head = im_header->get_data( ).

. . .

DATA: lv_zzfld TYPE zebeln

,read_eblen_from_ekko_sy_subrc TYPE sy-subrc

,ls_headx TYPE mepoheaderx

,lo_header_ref2cl TYPE REF TO cl_po_header_handle_mm .

 

SELECT SINGLE zzfld FROM ekko INTO lv_zzfld WHERE ebeln = ls_head-ebeln.

read_eblen_from_ekko_sy_subrc = sy-subrc.

 

DATA: lt_callstack_1 TYPE abap_callstack

,lt_callstack_2 TYPE sys_callst.

CALL FUNCTION 'SYSTEM_CALLSTACK'

EXPORTING

max_level = 0

IMPORTING

callstack = lt_callstack_1

et_callstack = lt_callstack_2.

 

READ TABLE lt_callstack_1 INTO DATA(ls_callstack_1) WITH KEY blockname = 'BAPI_PO_CHANGE'.

IF sy-subrc NE 0. "IF the code is not called from BAPI_PO_CHANGE

IF me->m_zzfld_old_val <> lv_zzfld.

IF read_eblen_from_ekko_sy_subrc EQ 0 AND lv_zzfld IS NOT INITIAL.

ls_head-zzfld = lv_zzfld.

lo_header_ref2cl ?= im_header.

lo_header_ref2cl->get_datax( IMPORTING ex_data = ls_headx ).

me->m_zzfld_old_val = ls_head-zzfld.

lo_header_ref2cl->my_ibs_firewall_on = 'X'.

lo_header_ref2cl->my_cust_firewall_on = 'X'.

ls_headx-zzfld = 'X'.

lo_header_ref2cl->set_datax( im_data = ls_headx ).

CALL METHOD im_header->set_data EXPORTING im_data = ls_head.

im_header->set_changed( ). "update the changes also in DB

ENDIF.

ENDIF.

ENDIF.

I tried moving the above update code to the POST method, but that did not resolve the issue either.

The only solution that worked was creating a Function Module (FM) that performs a direct update on the Z-field and calling it from the IF_EX_ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST~POST method.

Is this a good solution? Isn't it risky to write a direct UPDATE statement to a standard SAP table?

FM => ZUPD_ZZ_FLD:

  1. In the Attributes tab, under Processing Type, select: Update Module
  2. Underneath, select: Start Immediately
  3. In the Import tab, define two parameters (ensuring Pass Value is checked for both):
    • IM_EBELN TYPE EBELN
    • IM_ZZ_FLD TYPE [Your Field Type]
  4. In the Source Code tab, write the following code:
FUNCTION ZUPD_ZZ_FLD.

UPDATE ekko SET zz_fld = im_zz_fld WHERE ebeln = im_ebeln.

ENDFUNCTION.

METHOD if_ex_me_process_po_cust~post.

DATA: ls_mepoheader TYPE mepoheader.

me->m_zz_fld_old_val = 450001234 .

IF m_zz_fld_old_val IS NOT INITIAL AND im_header IS BOUND.

ls_mepoheader = im_header->get_data( ).

IF ls_mepoheader-ebeln IS NOT INITIAL.

CALL FUNCTION 'ZUPD_ZZ_FLD' IN UPDATE TASK

EXPORTING

im_ebeln = ls_mepoheader-ebeln

im_zz_fld = m_zz_fld_old_val.

CLEAR m_zz_fld_old_val.

ENDIF.

ENDIF.

 

Thankyou,

Hagit

entry.title: Extend MD_BUSINESSPARTNER_SRV with a additional ValueHelp
entry.content:
Hello dear forum,

We are currently in the middle of migrating from ECC to S/4 (brownfield approach). We want to use the app Manage Business Partner Master Data and have encountered an issue. The field AcctsReceivablePledgingCode (Table: TCESSION – Field: CESSION_KZ) is not visible in the UI by default, but as described in Note 3462395, it can be made visible via UI adaptation – which we have already done. Unfortunately, it appears as a free-text field without an associated value help.

Therefore, we attempted to create an interface and a corresponding consumption view for the value help and to added this via Metadata Extension to I_BusinessPartnerCustCoTP. 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZIPLEDINGIND'
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Interface view Pleding Indicator'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true


@AbapCatalog.preserveKey:true
@VDM.viewType: #BASIC

@ObjectModel: { dataCategory: #VALUE_HELP,
                representativeKey: 'AcctsReceivablePledgingCode',
                usageType.sizeCategory: #S,
                usageType.dataClass: #ORGANIZATIONAL,
                usageType.serviceQuality: #A,
                supportedCapabilities: [#VALUE_HELP_PROVIDER, #SEARCHABLE_ENTITY],
                modelingPattern: #VALUE_HELP_PROVIDER }


@ClientHandling.algorithm: #SESSION_VARIABLE

@Search.searchable: true
@Consumption.ranked: true



define view ZI_PledingIndicator
  as select from tcession
    inner join   tcessiont on  tcession.cession_kz = tcessiont.cession_kz
                           and tcession.company    = tcessiont.company
{
  tcession.company        as CompanyCode,
  @search.defaultSearchElement: true
  @search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
  @search.ranking: #HIGH
  tcession.cession_kz as AcctsReceivablePledgingCode,
  @Semantics.text: true
  @search: { defaultSearchElement: true, ranking: #LOW }
  @search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
  tcessiont.cession_text  as Text
}
where
  tcessiont.langu = $session.system_language;
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZCPLEDINGIND'
@ObjectModel.representativeKey: 'AcctsReceivablePledgingCode'
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@VDM.viewType: #CONSUMPTION
@Search.searchable: true

@ObjectModel.usageType.serviceQuality: #C
@ObjectModel.usageType.sizeCategory: #XL
@ObjectModel.usageType.dataClass: #MIXED
@EndUserText.label: 'Consumption View Pleding Indicator'


define view ZC_PLEDINGINDICATOR as select from ZI_PledingIndicator
association [0..1] to I_CompanyCode as _CompanyCode on  $projection.CompanyCode = _CompanyCode.CompanyCode
{
     @search.defaultSearchElement: true
       @search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
       @search.ranking: #HIGH
       @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_CompanyCode'
   key CompanyCode,
    @search.defaultSearchElement: true
       @search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
       @search.ranking: #HIGH
       @ObjectModel.text.element: ['Text']
   key AcctsReceivablePledgingCode,
    Text,
    _CompanyCode
}
@Metadata.layer: #CUSTOMER
annotate entity C_BusinessPartnerCustCo
    with 
{
   @EndUserText.label: 'AR Pledging Indicator (Test)'
   @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{entity: { name: 'ZC_PLEDINGINDICATOR', element: 'AcctsReceivablePledgingCode'},
                                  additionalBinding: [{element: 'CompanyCode', localElement: 'CompanyCodeForEdit', usage: #FILTER}]}]
  AcctsReceivablePledgingCode;
    
}

 

However, the value help does not appear in the app, but changing End-User Labels is working fine for example.

Is the value help not showing up, because it is not part of the underlying virtual data model?

If yes, do you have any ideas on how we can implement this?

Unfortunately, this field is linked to many processes and additional custom codings on our side.

Thank you in advance for your support!

Regards 

Kai

entry.title: Bug in ABAP Language
entry.content:
I just discovered my first bug in the ABAP Programming language introduced with SAP NetWeaver 7.4.

Some of our customers are still on very old releases and the bug just came up when they upgraded their system.

Prior to SAP NetWeaver 7.4, you were able to do the following:

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <result> type USR02.
DATA: results type table of USR02.

append initial line to results assigning <result>.

select * from usr02 into corresponding fields of <result>.
  append initial line to results assigning <result>.
endselect.

This code does exactly what you would expect it to do on a release lower than NW 7.4 - It appends all entries of USR02 to the table RESULTS.

However, starting with NW 7.4 the following happens:

  1. An initial line is appended to RESULTS and <RESULT> is assigned. <RESULT> now points to the first row of RESULTS.
  2. <RESULT> is populated with the first row of USR02, therefore the first row of RESULTS now also contains the data of USR02.
  3. A new initial line is appended to RESULTS and <RESULT> is assigned. <RESULT> now points to the second row of RESULTS.
  4. A new row of USR02 is selected. <RESULT> does not get populated, however the first row of RESULTS gets changed. The second row of RESULTS remains empty.

...

This is clearly a bug.

My guess:

Prior to NW 7.4, a pointer was passed to the select, now, the field symbol is no longer handled as a pointer within the select, instead the address of the work area is passed to the select.

This is confusing behaviour, as the same field symbols used in INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF and between SELECT and ENDSELECT now point to different addresses. This should not happen.

I suggest, that either the compiler will error when having to compile this, or that the previous, correct behaviour is restored.

entry.title: GOS Attachments Migration from SAP ECC to S/4HANA and Storage in SharePoint
entry.content:
We are migrating from SAP ECC to S/4HANA. Currently, GOS attachments for FB03 are stored in SAP tables such as SOOD, SOFFPHIO, and SOFFCONT1.

Our requirement is to store all new GOS attachments directly in SharePoint instead of these SAP tables.

In addition, we would like to migrate existing GOS attachments from SAP to SharePoint while maintaining the attachment links and accessibility from FB03.

Has anyone implemented a similar solution? What is the recommended approach, tools, or SAP-supported integration for storing GOS attachments in SharePoint and migrating historical attachments?

Any guidance or best practices would be appreciated.

entry.title: Issue with Dynpro Screen Download/Upload Between Systems After Namespace Change
entry.content:
We migrated a custom SAP development from System A to System B and changed the namespace during the process.

Example:
/ABC/PROGRAM → YXYZ_PROGRAM

Most objects work fine after renaming. However, when we download a Dynpro/screen from System A and upload it into System B, we get a “screen overflow” error. Even after updating all obvious references to the new namespace, the screen still doesn’t work correctly.


Has anyone encountered screen overflow or Dynpro upload issues after a namespace change? Are there hidden dependencies in screens that don’t get updated automatically during download/upload?
We migrated a custom SAP development from System A to System B and changed the namespace during the process.

Example:
/ABC/PROGRAM → YXYZ_PROGRAM

Most objects work fine after renaming. However, when we download a Dynpro/screen from System A and upload it into System B, we get a “screen overflow” error. Even after updating all obvious references to the new namespace, the screen still doesn’t work correctly.

Has anyone encountered screen overflow or Dynpro upload issues after a namespace change? Are there hidden dependencies in screens that don’t get updated automatically during download/upload?

entry.title: RPR_ABAP_SOURCE_SCAN Does Not Search Text Elements / Text Symbols
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

While analyzing a requirement to identify occurrences of a company name in custom developments, I noticed that the standard report RPR_ABAP_SOURCE_SCAN does not return results when the text is maintained in program Text Elements (Text Symbols).

Example:

- A company name exists in a Text Symbol of a custom program.
- Running RPR_ABAP_SOURCE_SCAN with the company name as the search string returns no results.
- The same text is found when reading the program text pool using READ TEXTPOOL.

My understanding is that RPR_ABAP_SOURCE_SCAN scans ABAP source code but does not search Text Pools (Text Symbols, Selection Texts, List Headers, etc.).

Questions:

1. Is this an expected limitation of RPR_ABAP_SOURCE_SCAN?
2. Is there any SAP standard tool that can search Text Elements across programs?
3. How do you typically search for a text maintained in Text Symbols across custom developments?

As a workaround, I created a utility report using READ TEXTPOOL to scan text symbols in custom programs and it successfully identifies the occurrences.

Looking forward to your insights.

Thanks,
Aravindhan Chandran

entry.title: Custom Fields Working in Create CR but Not in Change CR – Missing LT_MAPPINGS Entry in MAP_DATAX_2AP
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have created new custom fields in KNA1 and added them to the MDG BP data model (BP_CUSGEN). The structures were regenerated and SMT mapping was maintained. During a Change Request, the field values are correctly populated in ER_T_DATA_UPD and the corresponding DATAX indicators are also set in CL_USMD_DELTA_BUFFER=>CALC_DELTA within the USMD_DATAX structure.

However, while debugging the method MAP_DATAX_2API, I noticed that my custom fields are not present in the LT_MAPPINGS  internal table. As a result, the fields are not being transferred correctly, and the changes are not updated in KNA1 during the change scenario. The create scenario works as expected.

entry.title: OBD HU packing dimensions not upating in TM status tab
entry.content:
Hi ,

We have developed a program to update the Outbound Delivery (OBD) HU packing dimensions using the function modules HU_GET_HUS, HU_HEADER_UPDATE and HU_PACKING_UPDATE.

However, the updated dimensions are reflected only in the SAP GUI application and are not being updated or displayed correctly in the Fiori application

Click freight unit item, it goes to friori app

in the below screen shot not updated the dimensions. but it's updated via VL02N.

 

Thank with Regards,

Vallamuthu M.

entry.title: Error in ADT-Plugin 3.60 when creating a new Data Definition
entry.content:
I encounter a NullPointerException in ADT-Plugin 3.60 when trying to create a new Data Definition by right-clicking on a package node and selecting 'New Data Definition'.

The error occurs because adtObject is null if no ABAP object is selected, only a structural package node.

As a work-around it is possible to "right-click" on an existing Data Definition

entry.title: BADI or User exit for CO40 - psttr Planned Order Start Date
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am looking for a suitable BAdI or user exit for CO40 and CO41.

Requirement: Validate and restrict the Planned Order Start Date - PSTTR during processing.

Advance Thanks

entry.title: eclipse/ADT
entry.content: I have a question for everyone. When the ADT plug-in of eclipse2026.6 version performs the request activation, the ATC verification fails. The previous version will jump directly to the view page of ATC Problems, but this version will not jump but a pop-up box will prompt that the request is locked. What is the problem? Can it be the same as before?

entry.title: How to Trigger Formula-Based MIC Calculation Programmatically via BAPI in SAP QM (QE11 Automation)
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am working on automating the QE11 inspection result recording process through BAPI in SAP QM, and I have encountered a challenge related to formula-based MIC calculation.

**Background:**
In QE11, we have an inspection operation with multiple characteristics (MICs). One specific MIC — let's call it MIC 0020 ("AC Total Yarn Fault") — is configured with a formula in QP03 that automatically calculates the sum of all recorded defect values. This calculation is triggered when the inspection lot is unlocked (put into process) and the result is locked after computation. The field is display-only in QE11, meaning it cannot be manually entered — SAP computes and populates it internally.

**What We Are Trying to Achieve:**
We are replicating this QE11 flow programmatically using the BAPI `BAPI_INSPOPER_RECORDRESULTS`. The defect recording and result mapping for other MICs are working correctly. However, for MIC 0020, since it is formula-driven and display-only, passing the value directly via the BAPI is not possible — SAP does not accept manual input for this characteristic.

**The Core Question:**
When the unlock/lock action is performed in QE11, SAP internally triggers a formula function module (likely from the `QEFC_*` or `QEFM_*` namespace) that computes the summed result and writes it back to the characteristic. We need to replicate this same backend calculation programmatically.

Specifically, we would like to know:
1. How do we identify the exact formula function module assigned to a formula-based MIC from QP03 configuration (table `QPMA`, field `FORMELNR`)?
2. What are the standard import/export parameters for such formula FMs, and how should they be called in ABAP after recording the other MIC results?
3. Does the formula FM write the computed value directly to inspection tables (`QAMR`/`QAMV`), or does it return the value for us to pass back via BAPI?

**System Details:**
- S/4HANA (QM Module)
- BAPI Used: `BAPI_INSPOPER_RECORDRESULTS`
- MIC Type: Formula-based, display-only, result = sum of all defect characteristics

Any guidance, sample code, or pointers to relevant SAP notes would be greatly appreciated.

Thankyou
